pattern: The parity of the starting number decides the winner: even numbers hand the first player a forced win, odd numbers a forced loss, which a small game-state DP confirms.
hints: Play the game for the first few values of n by hand and tabulate who wins.
A state n is winning if the mover can pick a proper divisor x leaving an opponent in a losing state n - x.
The pattern that emerges is purely about whether n is even or odd; prove it with the DP and the answer is a one-line parity check.

stress.description: random n across the whole range
